content:
First, observe that for large \(x\), if \(\deg f=n\) and the leading coefficient is \(a\), then
\[
f(x-1)+f(x)+f(x+1)\sim3a\,x^n,
\]
while
\[
\frac{f(x)^2}{2013x}\sim\frac{a^2x^{2n}}{2013x}=\frac{a^2}{2013}\,x^{2n-1}.
\]
Equating degrees \(n\) and \(2n-1\) forces \(n=1\).  Hence
\[
f(x)=Ax+B,\quad A\neq0.
\]
Plugging into the identity for \(x\neq0\),
\[
\bigl[A(x-1)+B\bigr]+\bigl[Ax+B\bigr]+\bigl[A(x+1)+B\bigr]
=3Ax+3B
\]
and
\[
\frac{(Ax+B)^2}{2013x}
=\frac{A^2x^2+2ABx+B^2}{2013x}
=\frac{A^2}{2013}\,x+\frac{2AB}{2013}+\frac{B^2}{2013x}.
\]
For these to agree as rational functions of \(x\), the \(1/x\)‐term must vanish, so \(B^2=0\implies B=0\).  Then
\[
3Ax=\frac{A^2}{2013}\,x
\quad\Longrightarrow\quad
3A=\frac{A^2}{2013}
\quad\Longrightarrow\quad
A=6039
\]
(since \(A\neq0\)).  Thus
\[
f(x)=6039x,\quad f(1)=6039.
\]
There is exactly one nonconstant solution, so the sum of all possible values of \(f(1)\) is
\[
\boxed{6039}.
\]

message.content:
First, observe that for large \(x\), if \(\deg f=n\) and the leading coefficient is \(a\), then
\[
f(x-1)+f(x)+f(x+1)\sim3a\,x^n,
\]
while
\[
\frac{f(x)^2}{2013x}\sim\frac{a^2x^{2n}}{2013x}=\frac{a^2}{2013}\,x^{2n-1}.
\]
Equating degrees \(n\) and \(2n-1\) forces \(n=1\).  Hence
\[
f(x)=Ax+B,\quad A\neq0.
\]
Plugging into the identity for \(x\neq0\),
\[
\bigl[A(x-1)+B\bigr]+\bigl[Ax+B\bigr]+\bigl[A(x+1)+B\bigr]
=3Ax+3B
\]
and
\[
\frac{(Ax+B)^2}{2013x}
=\frac{A^2x^2+2ABx+B^2}{2013x}
=\frac{A^2}{2013}\,x+\frac{2AB}{2013}+\frac{B^2}{2013x}.
\]
For these to agree as rational functions of \(x\), the \(1/x\)‐term must vanish, so \(B^2=0\implies B=0\).  Then
\[
3Ax=\frac{A^2}{2013}\,x
\quad\Longrightarrow\quad
3A=\frac{A^2}{2013}
\quad\Longrightarrow\quad
A=6039
\]
(since \(A\neq0\)).  Thus
\[
f(x)=6039x,\quad f(1)=6039.
\]
There is exactly one nonconstant solution, so the sum of all possible values of \(f(1)\) is
\[
\boxed{6039}.
\]
